 {"id":"gt-jsup","title":"gt sling: patrol spawning must use wisp storage, not main DB","description":"**P0 Launch Blocker**

gt sling to patrol roles (witness/refinery/deacon) currently spawns patrols into the main beads database instead of wisp storage. This will pollute the permanent database with ephemeral patrol cycles.

**Root cause:**
spawnMoleculeFromProto() with IsWisp=true uses `--db .beads-wisp/beads.db`, but the patrol TEMPLATE (e.g., gt-qp2w for mol-witness-patrol) only exists in the main database. So bd mol run fails with 'no issue found'.

**The fix:**
bd mol run needs to support reading the template from the main DB while writing the spawned instance to wisp storage. This is a cross-database spawn operation.

Options:
1. Add `--template-db` flag to bd mol run (read template from here, write instance to --db)
2. Copy template to wisp DB before spawning
3. Make bd mol run automatically check main DB for templates when using wisp storage

**Workaround applied (MUST REVERT):**
Set IsWisp=false in slingToWitness/slingToRefinery/slingToDeacon patrol spawning.

**Files:**
- internal/cmd/sling.go: lines 680, 883, 1050 (IsWisp: false)
- Needs fix in bd mol run (beads repo)","status":"tombstone","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-23T16:30:56.128658-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:45.469219-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:45.469219-08:00","deleted_by":"daemon","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"bug"}

 {"id":"gt-xheo6","title":"Cross-rig worktree support for crew","description":"Enable crew workers to work on other rigs without losing identity.

**Problem**: When gastown/crew/joe needs to fix a beads bug, where do they work?
Current options are all problematic: mayor's rig (coordination, not coding), random
crew clone (conflicts), community repo (file locks), dogs (wrong abstraction).

**Solution**: Crew creates worktrees in target rigs with predictable paths.

Directory structure:
```
~/gt/<target-rig>/crew/<source-rig>-<name>/
~/gt/beads/crew/gastown-joe/     # joe from gastown working on beads
~/gt/gastown/crew/beads-wolf/    # wolf from beads working on gastown
```

Key principles:
- Identity preserved: BD_ACTOR stays gastown/crew/joe
- No conflicts: Each crew has own worktree
- No indirection: Direct work, no delegation to dogs
- Persistent: Worktree survives sessions (matches crew lifecycle)
- Dogs remain for Deacon's infrastructure work only

Commands:
- gt worktree <rig> - Create/enter worktree for rig
- gt worktree list - Show your worktrees  
- gt worktree remove <rig> - Clean up worktree","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-30T17:40:29.036817-08:00","created_by":"gastown/crew/joe","updated_at":"2025-12-30T17:40:29.036817-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-xheo6.1","title":"Implement gt worktree create command","description":"Create worktree in target rig for cross-rig work.

Usage: gt worktree <rig>

Behavior:
1. Detect current identity (gastown/crew/joe)
2. Compute worktree path: ~/gt/<rig>/crew/<source-rig>-<name>/
3. If worktree doesn't exist:
   - git worktree add <path> main
   - Set up .git config for identity preservation
4. cd to worktree (or print path for user to cd)
5. Preserve BD_ACTOR, GT_ROLE, etc.

Edge cases:
- Worktree already exists → just enter it
- Rig doesn't exist → error with helpful message
- No crew identity → error (must be crew to use this)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"gastown/polecats/dementus","created_at":"2025-12-30T17:40:48.552114-08:00","created_by":"gastown/crew/joe","updated_at":"2025-12-30T18:01:38.977513-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T18:01:38.977513-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented gt worktree command for cross-rig work","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-xheo6.1","depends_on_id":"gt-xheo6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T17:40:48.552745-08:00","created_by":"gastown/crew/joe"}]}

```
  mayor     ~/gt/mayor/crew/gastown-joe/     (2 uncommitted)
```

3. Clean up directory if needed

Safety:
- Refuse if uncommitted changes (unless --force)
- Confirm before removal","status":"hooked","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"gastown/polecats/toast","created_at":"2025-12-30T17:40:50.857156-08:00","created_by":"gastown/crew/joe","updated_at":"2025-12-30T17:57:19.066527-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-xheo6.3","depends_on_id":"gt-xheo6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T17:40:50.857664-08:00","created_by":"gastown/crew/joe"}]}

 {"id":"gt-xj9e5","title":"ZFC #7: Make message age filtering configurable","description":"**ZFC Violation:** internal/daemon/lifecycle.go:64-75

Go hardcodes 6-hour max message age:
```go
const MaxLifecycleMessageAge = 6 * time.Hour
```

**ZFC-compliant solution:**
- Make this configurable per message type
- Or let agents decide message relevance
- Low priority - not blocking liftoff

Reference: ~/gt/docs/zfc-violations-audit.md #7","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-27T21:32:54.911989-08:00","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2025-12-27T21:32:54.911989-08:00"}
